# UC-22.3.38 · DORA ICT Third-Party Risk Register — Inherent vs Residual Risk Score Reconciliation

## Description

Risk registers should show mitigation effect. Detecting rows where residual risk equals inherent without documented controls, or where math is inconsistent, protects the integrity of supervisory reporting derived from the register.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for DORA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes risk and sourcetypes dora:tprr. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Define scoring scale 1–25; (2) require control linkage from GRC; (3) quarterly risk committee review of hits; (4) exclude not-yet-assessed new vendors for 90 days via `onboarding_flag`; (5) export for internal audit. Indexes required: `index=risk`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=dora:tprr`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect, GRC export.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=risk` `sourcetype="dora:tprr"` (provider_id, inherent, residual, control_effectiveness, last_review).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Define scoring scale 1–25; (2) require control linkage from GRC; (3) quarterly risk committee review of hits; (4) exclude not-yet-assessed new vendors for 90 days via `onboarding_flag`; (5) export for internal audit.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=risk sourcetype="dora:tprr" earliest=-1d
| where inherent>0 AND residual=inherent AND (isnull(control_effectiveness) OR control_effectiveness="unknown")
| eval last=strptime(last_review,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where last<relative_time(now(),"-180d@d")
| table provider_id, inherent, residual, control_effectiveness, last_review
```

Understanding this SPL

**DORA ICT Third-Party Risk Register — Inherent vs Residual Risk Score Reconciliation** — Risk registers should show mitigation effect. Detecting rows where residual risk equals inherent without documented controls, or where math is inconsistent, protects the integrity of supervisory reporting derived from the register.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=risk` `sourcetype="dora:tprr"` (provider_id, inherent, residual, control_effectiveness, last_review). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect, GRC export. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: risk; **sourcetype**: dora:tprr. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=risk, sourcetype="dora:tprr", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where inherent>0 AND residual=inherent AND (isnull(control_effectiveness) OR control_effectiveness="unknown")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **last** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where last<relative_time(now(),"-180d@d")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: control_effectiveness, inherent, last, last_review, provider_id, residual.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ES Risk framework views (index=risk) and the ServiceNow GRC console for the same period so risk scores and asset fields line up.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (anomalies), Scatter (inherent vs residual), Single value (count).

## SPL

```spl
index=risk sourcetype="dora:tprr" earliest=-1d
| where inherent>0 AND residual=inherent AND (isnull(control_effectiveness) OR control_effectiveness="unknown")
| eval last=strptime(last_review,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where last<relative_time(now(),"-180d@d")
| table provider_id, inherent, residual, control_effectiveness, last_review
```

## Visualization

Table (anomalies), Scatter (inherent vs residual), Single value (count).
